raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Validate that the narrative (e.g., “consistent SOTA across all benchmarks,” causal attribution to the module) follows logically from the per-benchmark outcomes and ablation reference.",
    "R1.3": "Check that the reported mIoU/PQ values and stated deltas substantively support each claim, including SOTA status and generality.",
    "RX.1": "Ensure limits and uncertainty (e.g., matched SOTA, single ablation average, cross-domain generalization) are signposted.",
    "RX.2": "Guard against unsupported superlatives or scope overreach (e.g., “strongest unified segmentation model,” deployment claims).",
    "RX.3": "Keep work confined to validating solidity of the results passage without expanding into redesign or new analyses.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid language that could encourage premature or risky deployment in sensitive domains (e.g., medical imaging) based solely on these results.",
    "RX.5": "Preserve traceability of metrics, baselines, and section references so readers can verify and reproduce the evidence."
  }
}

rationale.R1.2: Validate that the narrative (e.g., “consistent SOTA across all benchmarks,” causal attribution to the module) follows logically from the per-benchmark outcomes and ablation reference.
rationale.R1.3: Check that the reported mIoU/PQ values and stated deltas substantively support each claim, including SOTA status and generality.
rationale.RX.1: Ensure limits and uncertainty (e.g., matched SOTA, single ablation average, cross-domain generalization) are signposted.
rationale.RX.2: Guard against unsupported superlatives or scope overreach (e.g., “strongest unified segmentation model,” deployment claims).
rationale.RX.3: Keep work confined to validating solidity of the results passage without expanding into redesign or new analyses.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid language that could encourage premature or risky deployment in sensitive domains (e.g., medical imaging) based solely on these results.
rationale.RX.5: Preserve traceability of metrics, baselines, and section references so readers can verify and reproduce the evidence.
